title: EU AI Act Annex IV — Technical Documentation Pack
subtitle: Deployment-governance records produced by Dativo Talon from signed runtime evidence and declared facts

sections.heading: 1. General description of the AI system (Annex IV s.1)
sections.body: Declared description of the system and the models/providers actually observed in signed runtime evidence for the selected scope.

rows: LLM assistant for support ticket triage
Summarize and route inbound support tickets

sections.heading: 3. Monitoring, functioning and control (Annex IV s.3, Art. 14 human oversight)
sections.body: Every governed request produces an HMAC-signed evidence record (policy decision, classification, model, cost, duration) exported via OpenTelemetry. Operators monitor via the dashboard and metrics endpoints; plan-review gates require human approval before agent tool execution where configured. Declared oversight arrangements: Support lead reviews flagged tickets daily

sections.heading: 5. Risk management system (Annex IV s.5, Art. 9)
sections.body: Risk-management controls Talon applies at deployment time (EU AI Act Art. 9 support). Enforcement outcomes for the selected scope are quantified in section 3. Denial reasons recorded in evidence:

rows: Every request evaluated against .talon.yaml policy via embedded OPA before execution
EU identifier patterns scanned on input and output; redact/block per policy
Attachment content sandboxed in isolation delimiters; instruction patterns scanned
Per-request, daily, and monthly budgets enforced per tenant
Tier-based routing and egress rules restricting destinations by region
Agent learnings constrained by category allowlists, PII scanning, and audit records

sections.heading: 6. Changes through the lifecycle (Annex IV s.6)
sections.body: Policy files carry a canonical version hash recorded in every evidence record (policy_version), making policy changes traceable across the system lifecycle. Governed agent memory writes are individually audited.

sections.heading: 9. Post-market monitoring (Annex IV s.9, Art. 72)
sections.body: Talon's evidence store provides continuous post-deployment records: every governed request is signed at write time and retained per the configured retention policy, supporting the post-market monitoring plan (Art. 72). Scope covered: 3 records from 2026-03-01 to 2026-05-20.

sections.heading: Items to complete outside Talon
sections.body: Talon governs deployment and produces runtime records; it is not the model provider. The following Annex IV items must be completed by your organisation and/or the model provider, and attached to this pack.

rows: 2. Development process of the underlying model(s)

claim_note: This document contains supporting records and control descriptions for EU AI Act Annex IV technical documentation, generated from HMAC-signed runtime evidence and operator-declared facts. It is not a completed legal filing, a certification, or a compliance determination. Review with your DPO or counsel. See LIMITATIONS.md.
